source_files.role: large command and boundary regression suite

split_boundaries.responsibility: App, Runner, top-level dispatch, and help
split_boundaries.guard: command catalog and runner injection tests

split_boundaries.responsibility: Mission aggregate, status, next, artifacts, history, dashboard, readiness, gateway, evidence
Foundry, Atlas, Pulse, Blueprint compatibility, complex-refactor, and live-mutation readbacks
Foundry process invocation and JSON output
split_boundaries.guard: runner interface and no-provider default tests

architectural_boundary: Command should become a thin client over Mission and control-plane APIs; domain validation and readiness truth should remain owned by the producing authority.
duplication_findings: Command contains broad readback and compatibility logic for Mission, Foundry, Atlas, Pulse, Blueprint, live mutation, and RSI.
Command tests encode many domain contracts locally, increasing drift risk when upstream schemas change.
The Runner abstraction is a useful boundary for replacing direct subprocess calls with a versioned client later.
refactor_order: delete duplicated domain truth only after consumer contract tests pass
non_goals: do not claim RSI or mutation authority
